eval-criteria-1.content:
Evaluate cross-artifact completeness:

- **Capability Coverage**: Flag functional.md capabilities that lack corresponding technical design (components, interfaces, or data flow)
- **What-Changes Coverage**: Flag "What Changes" items in functional.md without a technical counterpart explaining how the change is realized
- **Orphan Design**: Flag technical.md sections that don't trace back to any functional capability or change

When citing gaps, name the specific capability and the missing technical element.

severity-cal-1.content:
- **HIGH**: Blocks implementation or causes incorrect behavior (e.g., functional capability with zero technical design — no CMP-*, no interface, no data flow)
- **MEDIUM**: Degrades functionality or creates ambiguity that will require rework
- **LOW**: Minor issue, style, or nitpick that won't block progress
severity-cal-1.justification: Adds concrete calibration example for HIGH severity based on confirmed evaluation — zero technical design forces implementers to invent architecture
severity-cal-1.evidence: developer-views-status had no CMP-*, no interface, no data flow; evaluator confirmed HIGH as appropriate

evaluation-criteria-00003.content: Enumerate source artifact items, then verify each has a target counterpart
evaluation-criteria-00003.justification: Enumeration-first method catches omissions that holistic reading misses — absences are invisible without an explicit checklist
evaluation-criteria-00003.evidence: Critic found developer-views-status gap by iterating functional capabilities against CMP-* components in technical.md

evaluation-criteria-00004.content: Prioritize finding precision over volume — one correct finding beats padded lists
evaluation-criteria-00004.justification: 1/1 useful findings scored 100% effectiveness; padding with low-quality findings would reduce credibility
evaluation-criteria-00004.evidence: Evaluator rated single high-quality finding as 100% effectiveness despite noting thoroughness cannot be assessed
